speaker: COMINIUS
text: If I should tell thee o’er this thy day’s work,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Thou ’t not believe thy deeds. But I’ll report it

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Where senators shall mingle tears with smiles;

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Where great patricians shall attend and shrug,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: I’ th’ end admire; where ladies shall be frighted

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And, gladly quaked, hear more; where the dull tribunes,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: That with the fusty plebeians hate thine honors,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Shall say against their hearts “We thank the gods

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Our Rome hath such a soldier.”

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Yet cam’st thou to a morsel of this feast,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Having fully dined before.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: O general,

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Here is the steed, we the caparison.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Hadst thou beheld—

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Pray now, no more. My mother,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Who has a charter to extol her blood,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: When she does praise me grieves me. I have done

speaker: MARTIUS
text: As you have done—that’s what I can;

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Induced as you have been—that’s for my country.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: He that has but effected his good will

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Hath overta’en mine act.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: You shall not be

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The grave of your deserving. Rome must know

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The value of her own. ’Twere a concealment

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Worse than a theft, no less than a traducement,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: To hide your doings and to silence that

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Which, to the spire and top of praises vouched,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Would seem but modest. Therefore, I beseech you—

speaker: COMINIUS
text: In sign of what you are, not to reward

speaker: COMINIUS
text: What you have done—before our army hear me.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: I have some wounds upon me, and they smart

speaker: MARTIUS
text: To hear themselves remembered.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Should they not,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Well might they fester ’gainst ingratitude

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And tent themselves with death. Of all the horses—

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Whereof we have ta’en good and good store—of all

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The treasure in this field achieved and city,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: We render you the tenth, to be ta’en forth

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Before the common distribution

speaker: COMINIUS
text: At your only choice.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: I thank you, general,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: But cannot make my heart consent to take

speaker: MARTIUS
text: A bribe to pay my sword. I do refuse it

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And stand upon my common part with those

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That have beheld the doing.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: May these same instruments, which you profane,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Never sound more! When drums and trumpets shall

speaker: MARTIUS
text: I’ th’ field prove flatterers, let courts and cities be

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Made all of false-faced soothing! When steel grows

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Soft as the parasite’s silk, let him be made

speaker: MARTIUS
text: An ovator for th’ wars! No more, I say.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: For that I have not washed my nose that bled,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Or foiled some debile wretch—which, without note,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Here’s many else have done—you shout me forth

speaker: MARTIUS
text: In acclamations hyperbolical,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: As if I loved my little should be dieted

speaker: MARTIUS
text: In praises sauced with lies.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Too modest are you,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: More cruel to your good report than grateful

speaker: COMINIUS
text: To us that give you truly. By your patience,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: If ’gainst yourself you be incensed, we’ll put you,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Like one that means his proper harm, in manacles,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Then reason safely with you. Therefore be it known,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: As to us to all the world, that Caius Martius

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Wears this war’s garland, in token of the which

speaker: COMINIUS
text: My noble steed, known to the camp, I give him,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: With all his trim belonging. And from this time,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: For what he did before Corioles, call him,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: With all th’ applause and clamor of the host,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Martius Caius Coriolanus! Bear

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Th’ addition nobly ever!

speaker: ALL
text: Martius Caius Coriolanus!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I will go wash;

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And when my face is fair, you shall perceive

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Whether I blush or no. Howbeit, I thank you.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I mean to stride your steed and at all times

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To undercrest your good addition

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To th’ fairness of my power.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: So, to our tent,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Where, ere we do repose us, we will write

speaker: COMINIUS
text: To Rome of our success.—You, Titus Lartius,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Must to Corioles back. Send us to Rome

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The best, with whom we may articulate

speaker: COMINIUS
text: For their own good and ours.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: I shall, my lord.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The gods begin to mock me. I, that now

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Refused most princely gifts, am bound to beg

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Of my lord general.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Take ’t, ’tis yours. What is ’t?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I sometime lay here in Corioles

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: At a poor man’s house; he used me kindly.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: He cried to me; I saw him prisoner;

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: But then Aufidius was within my view,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And wrath o’erwhelmed my pity. I request you

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To give my poor host freedom.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: O, well begged!

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Were he the butcher of my son, he should

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Be free as is the wind.—Deliver him, Titus.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Martius, his name?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: By Jupiter, forgot!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I am weary; yea, my memory is tired.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Have we no wine here?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Go we to our tent.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The blood upon your visage dries; ’tis time

speaker: COMINIUS
text: It should be looked to. Come.
